content: Just as the spelling variant 'reflexion' (coexisting alongside the more common 'reflection' in English language pedagogy) retains an alternate, older form of a core concept that can offer divergent contextual perspective, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by retaining an older 'reflexion' copy of the model’s initial wrong reasoning trace alongside the refined corrected reasoning, to enable multi-perspective self-contrast. This persistent alternate 'reflexion' trace gives LLMs a tangible reference point to identify inconsistencies between naive and refined problem-solving, rather than discarding the initial trace entirely after reflection.
